tweets.title:
India’s indigenous loitering munition capability takes another step forward.

Hoverit successfully demonstrated the Divyastra Mk-1 tactical UAV before senior Indian Army officials in Jodhpur. Notably, the system was launched from a Made-in-India Thar-based mobile platform, highlighting growing domestic integration in India’s defence ecosystem.

- Vehicle-mounted rapid launch capability
- Up to 500 km operational range
- Up to 5 hours endurance
- ISR, reconnaissance & precision strike roles
- Configurable EO/IR payloads & mission-specific warheads
tweets.content:

tweets.title:
INDIAN NAVY REVEALS SCALE OF ‘OPERATION SINDOOR’ DEPLOYMENT

Following the Pahalgam terror attack, the Indian Navy rapidly deployed frontline warships, submarines, carrier battle groups, and aviation assets into the Arabian Sea, maintaining what Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi described as “constant pressure” on Pakistan. 

According to the Navy Chief: 
- A forward deployment in the Northern Arabian Sea was established within days
- Submarines, surface combatants, and carrier assets were placed on operational readiness
- Pakistan Navy was effectively forced to remain close to harbour and the Makran coast during the standoff 

Reports indicate that around 36 frontline warships and multiple submarines, led by an INS Vikrant carrier battle group, were deployed during the operation. 

Admiral Tripathi said the operation demonstrated the Navy’s combat readiness, operational reach, and deterrence capability, while also highlighting growing tri-service coordination among the Army, Navy, and Air Force. 

The deployment increased shipping risks and insurance costs around Pakistan’s maritime zone, adding economic pressure alongside the military posture.
tweets.content:
INDIAN NAVY REVEALS SCALE OF ‘OPERATION SINDOOR’ DEPLOYMENT

Following the Pahalgam terror attack, the Indian Navy rapidly deployed frontline warships, submarines, carrier battle groups, and aviation assets into the Arabian Sea, maintaining what Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi described as “constant pressure” on Pakistan.

According to the Navy Chief:
- A forward deployment in the Northern Arabian Sea was established within days
- Submarines, surface combatants, and carrier assets were placed on operational readiness
- Pakistan Navy was effectively forced to remain close to harbour and the Makran coast during the standoff

Reports indicate that around 36 frontline warships and multiple submarines, led by an INS Vikrant carrier battle group, were deployed during the operation.

Admiral Tripathi said the operation demonstrated the Navy’s combat readiness, operational reach, and deterrence capability, while also highlighting growing tri-service coordination among the Army, Navy, and Air Force.

The deployment increased shipping risks and insurance costs around Pakistan’s maritime zone, adding economic pressure alongside the military posture.

tweets.title:
US–Iran tensions escalate further

The U.S. says it struck Iranian military sites on the Gulf coast after Iran allegedly shot down a U.S. MQ-1 drone over international waters.

In response, Iran’s IRGC claims it targeted a U.S. airbase, while Kuwait reportedly activated air defenses amid missile and drone alerts.

Meanwhile, negotiations remain fragile as Tehran warns it will not accept any deal that compromises Iranian interests or control over the Strait of Hormuz.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
31st May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

●  Iran’s IRGC intercepted and destroyed a US MQ-1 drone after it entered Iranian territorial waters early Sunday.

● The IRGC Navy reported that 28 vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, were granted permits to transit the Strait in the past 24 hours.

● The IDF foiled a Hezbollah plot to destroy the Qaraoun Dam on the Litani River to flood the area and obstruct advances toward Beaufort Castle and Ali al-Taher Ridge.

● Iran’s Khatam al-Anbia Central Headquarters stated that the Armed Forces now exercise full control over the Strait of Hormuz and all vessels must follow Iranian regulations.

● Iranian Ambassador to Qatar Ali Saleh Abadi met with Russian Ambassador Dmitry Dugadin to discuss bilateral ties and regional issues.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 118 commercial vessels and disabled 5 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF
tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
31st May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran’s IRGC intercepted and destroyed a US MQ-1 drone after it entered Iranian territorial waters early Sunday.

● The IRGC Navy reported that 28 vessels, including oil tankers and container ships, were granted permits to transit the Strait in the past 24 hours.

● The IDF foiled a Hezbollah plot to destroy the Qaraoun Dam on the Litani River to flood the area and obstruct advances toward Beaufort Castle and Ali al-Taher Ridge.

● Iran’s Khatam al-Anbia Central Headquarters stated that the Armed Forces now exercise full control over the Strait of Hormuz and all vessels must follow Iranian regulations.

● Iranian Ambassador to Qatar Ali Saleh Abadi met with Russian Ambassador Dmitry Dugadin to discuss bilateral ties and regional issues.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 118 commercial vessels and disabled 5 to ensure compliance with the naval blockade.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
30th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● CENTCOM, via the UKMTO/JMIC, issued an urgent maritime advisory north of Oman’s Musandam Peninsula, warning mariners of imminent US naval operations to counter suspected Iranian mine-laying.

● In Washington, President Trump reviewed the draft peace deal with top security advisers in the Situation Room. The session concluded with zero announcements, keeping the region in an uneasy gridlock.

● Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the U.S. possesses more than sufficient stockpiles to immediately resume aggressive bombardment strikes if Tehran rejects core U.S. parameters, though officials maintain the ceasefire "is not officially over."

● Iranian FM Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei confirmed text is not finalized. Tehran remains strictly focused on ending hostilities and lifting the U.S. naval blockade, refusing to negotiate the finer details of its nuclear plan at this stage.

● Hezbollah launched a major rocket barrage deep into northern Israel targeting Safed and Kiryat Shmona. In response, the IDF issued immediate evacuation orders for 7 southern Lebanese villages as ground operations intensify.

● The IRGC Navy claimed that 20 commercial vessels successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours in direct coordination with Iranian forces.

END OF THE BRIEF

— https://nitter.net/NetramDefence/status/2060772031776620633#m

tweets.title:
Centre Deploys CRPF CoBRA Battalions to Manipur

The Ministry of Home Affairs has approved the deployment of two CRPF CoBRA battalions, 207 from West Bengal and 210 from Assam, for operational duties in Manipur.

The move comes as the Centre intensifies efforts to restore long-term stability in the violence-hit state, where ethnic clashes since May 2023 have claimed over 260 lives.

tweets.title:
General N. S. Raja Subramani Takes Charge as Chief of Defence Staff

With a career starting in 1985 in the Garhwal Rifles, the new CDS brings extensive experience from operational roles in Jammu & Kashmir and Assam, alongside senior positions as Vice Chief of the Army Staff and Military Adviser to the NSCS. He is highly decorated, having received the PVSM, AVSM, SM, and VSM for his service.

General Subramani’s mandate focuses on critical reforms within the Indian Armed Forces:
- Theaterisation: Implementing integrated military commands.
- Jointness: Enhancing synergies across the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
-Indigenization: Accelerating Atmanirbharta (self-reliance) in defense production.
tweets.content:
General N. S. Raja Subramani Takes Charge as Chief of Defence Staff

With a career starting in 1985 in the Garhwal Rifles, the new CDS brings extensive experience from operational roles in Jammu & Kashmir and Assam, alongside senior positions as Vice Chief of the Army Staff and Military Adviser to the NSCS. He is highly decorated, having received the PVSM, AVSM, SM, and VSM for his service.

General Subramani’s mandate focuses on critical reforms within the Indian Armed Forces:
- Theaterisation: Implementing integrated military commands.
- Jointness: Enhancing synergies across the Army, Navy, and Air Force.
-Indigenization: Accelerating Atmanirbharta (self-reliance) in defense production.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
30th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● CENTCOM, via the UKMTO/JMIC, issued an urgent maritime advisory north of Oman’s Musandam Peninsula, warning mariners of imminent US naval operations to counter suspected Iranian mine-laying.

● In Washington, President Trump reviewed the draft peace deal with top security advisers in the Situation Room. The session concluded with zero announcements, keeping the region in an uneasy gridlock.

● Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth stated the U.S. possesses more than sufficient stockpiles to immediately resume aggressive bombardment strikes if Tehran rejects core U.S. parameters, though officials maintain the ceasefire "is not officially over."

● Iranian FM Spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei confirmed text is not finalized. Tehran remains strictly focused on ending hostilities and lifting the U.S. naval blockade, refusing to negotiate the finer details of its nuclear plan at this stage.

● Hezbollah launched a major rocket barrage deep into northern Israel targeting Safed and Kiryat Shmona. In response, the IDF issued immediate evacuation orders for 7 southern Lebanese villages as ground operations intensify.

● The IRGC Navy claimed that 20 commercial vessels successfully transited the Strait of Hormuz over the past 24 hours in direct coordination with Iranian forces.

END OF THE BRIEF

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
29th May 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF eliminated Imad Hassan Hussein Aslim, the Deputy Commander of the Gaza Brigade and the Commander of the Zeitoun Battalion in Hamas' Military Wing.

● Speaker of the Iranian Parliament Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf stated that Iran will not take any action on current issues until the US makes the first move.

● Washington explicitly rejected a proposal from Muscat that would allow a joint Iran-Oman authority to levy commercial shipping fees in the Strait of Hormuz.

● Declassified satellite imagery confirmed 4 structural impacts from yesterday’s Iranian missile strike on Ali Al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait. CENTCOM stated the base remains fully operational.

● The bulk carrier MDL KAMRAN successfully crossed the Strait of Hormuz en route to Imam Khomeini port after returning from Brazil.

● As of May 29, CENTCOM has redirected 115 commercial vessels to ensure no commerce enters or leaves Iranian ports.

END OF THE BRIEF

tweets.title:
The current market demand is not for “Made in India” but for “Made in Pakistan.”: Bilawal Bhutto

A country that struggles to produce even an indigenous 100cc motorcycle engine is talking about building fighter jets and defence products. Simply putting a “Made in Pakistan” label(even the label printed in China) does not make a defence product truly indigenous.

A few days back, Pakistan’s Defence Minister Khawaja Asif also claimed that selling fighter jets would clear all loans and turn the country’s economy into a top-tier global economy.

How long will they keep selling such churan to their awam ?

Video credit: ARY news

tweets.title: India has officially finalized an agreement to supply BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles to Vietnam. Announced by Indian Defense Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, this deal makes Vietnam the third Southeast Asian nation to procure the missile system.
tweets.content: India has officially finalized an agreement to supply BrahMos supersonic cruise missiles to Vietnam. Announced by Indian Defense Secretary Rajesh Kumar Singh at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore, this deal makes Vietnam the third Southeast Asian nation to procure the missile system.
